whatThisIs: Check which Medicare enrollment window applies on a given date. Sophie Well covers the Initial Enrollment Period (the seven-month window straddling the 65th birthday month - three months before, the birthday month itself, and three months after), the General Enrollment Period (January 1 through March 31 each year for people who missed IEP), and the Part B Special Enrollment Period that opens for eight months after employer-sponsored coverage ends. Enter a birthdate and a target enrollment date, and the tool reports which window is open and how many days remain.
whenToUse: Use this when a patient is approaching 65 and asks which months they can enroll without penalty, when someone losing employer coverage needs to confirm their SEP runs eight months from coverage loss (not from COBRA election), or when reviewing whether a missed window will trigger the lifetime Part B late-enrollment penalty (10 percent per 12-month delay). Enrollment windows are unforgiving - the penalty for missing IEP without a qualifying SEP is permanent - so a 30-second window check is the easiest way to avoid an irreversible mistake.
